# IEO Project Template

This is the project template for the IEO course taught in the MSc
program on Bioinformatics for the Health Sciences at the Universitat
Pompeu Fabra. The vignette contains details on how to adapt it to your
own dataset.

## Deliverables

The GitHub repo for this project should contain the directory structure
given in this template without altering its filenames. You may add
additional files or directories if you need them. Object names in the R
code in `.Rmd` and `.R` files **should not** contain non-english
characters such as `ç` or `ñ` or accents such as `à`, `é`, etc. The
analysis of the data described in the HTML file produced by the vignette
source `vignettes/IEOprojectAnalysis.Rmd` should contain the following
sections:

- **Front matter:** This is the basic information of the project that
  should appear at the beginning and should consist of the following
  items: title of the project including at the end or as subtitle “IEO
  XXXX, MSc Bioinformatics, UPF” (where `XXXX` refers to the year of the
  edition of the course), names of the authors and date. Here the words
  *Front Matter* **should not** be included as section name. This
  information is provided in the [YAML
  header](https://yihui.org/rmarkdown-cookbook/rmarkdown-anatomy) of the
  R Markdown document of the vignette.
- **Abstract:** Summary of the question and the findings (max. 200
  words). This can be written within the YAML header, as shown in the
  vignette source code.
- **Introduction:** Description of the question and the data employed to
  answer it. Please remove the text in the template describing the
  package template and how to build the vignette.
- **Quality assessment:** R code interspersed with text, describing the
  quality assessment steps.
- **Differential expression:** R code interspersed with text, describing
  the differential expression analysis.
- **Functional analysis:** R code interspersed with text, describing the
  functional analysis.
- **Discussion:** summary of the findings, limitations of the study,
  ways in which this type of study could be improved in the future.
- **AI assistance:** description of any use of AI tools to help with the
  analysis, including the specific prompts used, the output obtained
  from those prompts, and if you directly accepted the output or you had
  to modify it, and why. If you have not used any AI tool, you should
  explicitly state that in this section.
- **References:** bibliographic references.

## AI assistance

You may use AI tools, preferably AI pair programming tools such as
GitHub Copilot, to help you with the analysis. If you do so, you should
describe in the “AI assistance” section of the resulting HTML file the
specific prompts used, the output obtained from those prompts, and if
you directly accepted that output or you had to modify it, and why. If
you have not used any AI tool, you should explicitly state that in this
section.

If you use AI tools, please take into account the following principles:

- **Responsibility:** You are responsible for the content of your
  project, including content generated with the help of AI tools. If
  that content is not correct, your grade will reflect that, even if the
  content was generated by an AI tool.

- **Transparency:** You should document the use of AI tools in the “AI
  assistance” section. Failure to do so will be considered a breach of
  academic integrity and will be penalized accordingly.

- **Learning:** You should understand the content generated with the
  help of AI tools. If you use an AI tool to generate code, you should
  be able to explain how that code works and why it is correct. You
  should not use AI tools to outsource your brain and learning
  experience.

## Submission procedure

This assignment has to be submitted using GitHub Classroom. This means
that you should have cloned the GitHub repo of this assignment from the
organization account for IEO in the corresponding academic year at
`https://github.com/FCB-XXXX`, where `XXXX` refers to the year of the
edition of the IEO course, using the submission link provided at the IEO
Moodle site.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`IEOproject-package`](https://ieomscbinfupf.github.io/IEOproject/reference/IEOproject-package.md)
  [`IEOproject`](https://ieomscbinfupf.github.io/IEOproject/reference/IEOproject-package.md)
  : IEOproject: The IEO project template
- [`add_tech_rep()`](https://ieomscbinfupf.github.io/IEOproject/reference/add_tech_rep.md)
  : Add technical replicates
